name: Espigantosa Waterfall via Traviesa and return via Tramarius - Posets-Maladeta Natural Park
name_original: Cascada de la Espigantosa por la Traviesa y vuelta por Tramarius — Parque Natural Posets-Maladeta
tips:
This Tourist Information Office is open during the months of July and August to offer the best recommendations for routes and places of interest in the immediate surroundings of the municipality of Sahún, as well as the Benasque valley and the Posets-Maladeta Natural Park in general.

Schedule (July and August):
Sunday to Thursday from 10 a.m. to 2:30 p.m.
Friday and Saturday from 10 a.m. to 2:30 p.m. and from 4:00 p.m. to 7:30 p.m.

You can call or write a WhatsApp with your query to +34 722 67 94 17

This path is a little difficult to find, especially going downhill, since the humidity of the ravine means that the vegetation is always very overgrown. It is easier to follow it in the uphill direction.

Beautiful and powerful waterfall that can be comfortably observed from the Pleta del Estalló, located at about 1,500 meters above sea level. This is also where the bus departing from Eriste arrives in the summer months. You can check the bus schedules at the tourist office or on the website of the town hall.

This bridge, originally Romanesque in style (not Roman) has been rebuilt on multiple occasions. It crosses the waters of the Eriste ravine and under it there are some deep pools where you can take a good bath, although the water temperature is not suitable for everyone.

At this point, the path that runs next to a ditch and that goes up from Eriste forks into two, with a flatter option that leads to the Tramarius Bridge (with the possibility of continuing to La Espigantosa and the Ángel Orús Refuge from there) or a much steeper one that leads to the wilder valley of Aigüeta de la Vall.

This small town in the Benasque valley experienced a real population boom during the construction of the hydroelectric power station, reaching up to 13 bars. Today there are only three, all part of the roadside hotels. It belongs to the City Council of Sahún/Saúnc.

waypoints.name: Junction of the Tramarius, Eriste, and La Ball trails
